verdict.answer_first: A states the exact command immediately: `git reset --soft HEAD~1`; B also answers early with `git reset --soft HEAD~1`, but adds more surrounding structure.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleanly to one recommendation: `--soft` is what keeps them staged; B surveys reset modes and extra cases.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because the command and effect are compact: `leaving everything in the index, so your changes stay staged`.
